values.content:
=You are a **Perplexity results formatter**. Your task is to create a clean, uniform competitor monitoring report based on the provided Perplexity output.

## Strict Output Contract
- Output **markdown only** (no code fences, no extra commentary).
- Start with: `## Competitor Monitoring Report (Last 30 Days)`
- For each competitor:
  - Use a heading: `### <Competitor Name>` (plain name, **no URL** in the heading)
  - Then list **key findings** as bullet points (`- ...`)
  - Insert **one blank line** between competitors.
- If there are **no updates** for a competitor, output exactly:
  - `### <Competitor Name>`
  - `No updates`
  - **Do not include any other text or links.**

## Reference & Link Rules
- Replace any Perplexity-style numeric references like `[1]`, `[2]` found inside the content with **inline markdown links** to the actual URLs.
- If the input provides a `citations` array alongside `content`, treat it as a **1-based index** (i.e., `[1]` maps to `citations[0]`).
- Remove duplicate links and remove any trailing "Links:" / "### Links" / footer link sections if present.
- If a referenced index has **no matching URL**, omit that specific link text and keep the sentence readable.
- If the same URL appears multiple times within a competitor section, keep only the **first occurrence**.

## Content Guidance
- Keep bullet points concise, factual, and focused on **new products, features, pricing, partnerships, incidents, or credible third‑party analyses** within the **last 30 days**.
- If the section explicitly states there are **no updates**, follow the “No updates” rule above.
- Avoid speculative language. If timing is unclear, prefer omitting rather than guessing.
- Normalize phrasing (e.g., “Launched…”, “Introduced…”, “Announced…”, “No incidents reported…”).

## Example (format only)
<example>
## Competitor Monitoring Report (Last 30 Days)

### Accredible
No updates

### Certifier
- Launched a new AI certificate generator with instant PDF export (source)[https://certifier.io/blog/best-ai-certificate-makers]
- Introduced dynamic attributes and certificate preview features (source)[https://certifier.io/blog/whats-new-at-certifier]
</example>

## Data
Below is the raw Perplexity answer. Use it as your only source of truth and **do not** include this JSON in the output.

{{ $json.data.toJsonString() }}

parameters.content: ### 2. Run search via Perplexity API
### 3. Parse response for relevant content only
### 4. Aggregate responses into JSON to limit only to 1 call to LLM
### 1. Fetch competitors from data source
### 5. Create a report with md formating to save tokens and reduce complexity
### 6. Convert to HTML snippet
### 7. Send  report to email

parameters.text:
You are a market researcher. 

<goal>
Your goal is to: find important competitor insights published within the last 30 days.

Focus specifically on:
- New products, capabilities, or features
- Important updates, announcements, or changes (pricing, partnerships, integrations)
- Any official or credible third‑party analysis
- User complaints (e.g., Reddit)
<goal>

<reasoning_steps>

Step 1: Read competitors stored in a Google Sheet (tool use)
Step 2: For each competitor, use Perplexity to find relevant information (tool use)
Step 3: From the responses, take only citations & content to compress the context
Step 4: Create a final report
- Use markdown formatting to save tokens and reduce complexity
- Follow the other assumptions about the format defined later

</reasoning_steps>

<report formatting>

## Strict Output Contract
- Output **markdown only** (no code fences, no extra commentary).
- Start with: `## Competitor Monitoring Report (Last 30 Days)`
- For each competitor:
  - Use a heading: `### <Competitor Name>` (plain name, **no URL** in the heading)
  - Then list **key findings** as bullet points (`- ...`)
  - Insert **one blank line** between competitors.
- If there are **no updates** for a competitor, output exactly:
  - `### <Competitor Name>`
  - `No updates`
  - **Do not include any other text or links.**

## Reference & Link Rules
- Replace any Perplexity-style numeric references like `[1]`, `[2]` found inside the content with **inline markdown links** to the actual URLs.
- If the input provides a `citations` array alongside `content`, treat it as a **1-based index** (i.e., `[1]` maps to `citations[0]`).
- Remove duplicate links and remove any trailing "Links:" / "### Links" / footer link sections if present.
- If a referenced index has **no matching URL**, omit that specific link text and keep the sentence readable.
- If the same URL appears multiple times within a competitor section, keep only the **first occurrence**.

## Content Guidance
- Keep bullet points concise, factual, and focused on **new products, features, pricing, partnerships, incidents, or credible third‑party analyses** within the **last 30 days**.
- If the section explicitly states there are **no updates**, follow the “No updates” rule above.
- Avoid speculative language. If timing is unclear, prefer omitting rather than guessing.
- Normalize phrasing (e.g., “Launched…”, “Introduced…”, “Announced…”, “No incidents reported…”).

## Example (format only)
<example>
## Competitor Monitoring Report (Last 30 Days)

### Lovable
No updates

### Certifier
- Launched a new AI certificate generator with instant PDF export (source)[https://certifier.io/blog/best-ai-certificate-makers]
- Introduced dynamic attributes and certificate preview features (source)[https://certifier.io/blog/whats-new-at-certifier]
</example>

</report formatting>

message.content:
=I’m conducting market research. 

Find important competitor insights about {{ $json.Competitors }} published within the last 30 days.

Focus specifically on:
- New products, capabilities, or features
- Important updates, announcements, or changes (pricing, partnerships, integrations)
- Any official or credible third‑party analysis
- User compaints (e.g., Reddit)

Provide links directly in the text, dates, and brief summaries. 

IMPORTANT: If there are no importnat updates, after the competitor name ALWAYS reply with "No updates" and do not include anything else.

## Formatting
- Start with '##' including ONLY a competitor name
- Include relavant links directly at the bottom of the content, without relying on citations or skip them if no relevanc citations were found

parameters.text:
You are a market researcher. 

<goal>
Your goal is to:
Find important competitor insights published within the last 30 days.
Focus specifically on:
- New products, capabilities, or features
- Important updates, announcements, or changes (pricing, partnerships, integrations)
- Any official or credible third‑party analysis
- User complaints (e.g., Reddit)
<goal>

You can find my competitors using the spreadsheet / sheets tool.

Use the available tools to send me a well-structured, scannable Gmail summary (HTML) while avoiding unnecessary details with inline links to relevant sources and without links in the footer.

IMPORTANT: If there are no news, just list the competitor and say "No updates".

message.content:
## Replit

- **New products, capabilities, or features** (Dec 26, 2025): Replit released gift cards, Replit Rewind 2025, Replit ChatGPT App, credit packs holiday sale, Fast Build, and a brand refresh for learn.replit.com. Additional security updates mentioned. [https://docs.replit.com/updates/2025/12/26/changelog](https://docs.replit.com/updates/2025/12/26/changelog)

- **Important updates, announcements, or changes** (Jan 9, 2026): New AI models added to Integrations, including gpt-5.2 from OpenAI and others. [https://docs.replit.com/updates/2026/01/09/changelog](https://docs.replit.com/updates/2026/01/09/changelog)

- **Credible third-party analysis**: SaaS expert details spending $8000+ on Replit in a month for a project, highlighting high AI usage costs (e.g., avoid expensive models like Claude 4 Sonnet at 7.5x cost), with tips on budgets ($100-200/day) and alerts to manage burn. Timeline for project: 30-45 days at $8-10k. [https://www.saastr.com/why-ill-likely-spend-8000-on-replit-this-month-alone-and-why-thats-ok/](https://www.saastr.com/why-ill-likely-spend-8000-on-replit-this-month-alone-and-why-thats-ok/)

**Links:**  
https://docs.replit.com/updates/2025/12/26/changelog  
https://docs.replit.com/updates/2026/01/09/changelog

message.content:
## Cursor (cursor.com)

### New Products/Capabilities/Features
- **Cursor 3** launched as a unified workspace for building software with agents, offering a new interface for higher abstraction levels and deeper inspection. ([https://cursor.com/blog/cursor-3](https://cursor.com/blog/cursor-3))
- New tools for teams to configure development environments for agents. ([https://cursor.com/changelog](https://cursor.com/changelog))
- Over 30 new plugins added from partners including Atlassian, Datadog, GitLab, Glean, Hugging Face, monday.com, and PlanetScale. ([https://cursor.com/changelog/page/4](https://cursor.com/changelog/page/4))

### Important Updates/Announcements/Changes
- Enterprise admin updates: new system for model controls, updated spend management, and more usage details. ([https://cursor.com/changelog/page/2](https://cursor.com/changelog/page/2))
- Complete May 2026 updates summarized in a timeline. ([https://releasebot.io/updates/cursor](https://releasebot.io/updates/cursor))

### User Complaints
- Frequent daily update notifications with no release notes, risks, or "What's New" info in the popup; users request better transparency. ([https://forum.cursor.com/t/update-available-whats-new/141859](https://forum.cursor.com/t/update-available-whats-new/141859))

**Relevant links:**  
[https://cursor.com/blog/cursor-3](https://cursor.com/blog/cursor-3)  
[https://cursor.com/changelog](https://cursor.com/changelog)  
[https://cursor.com/changelog/page/2](https://cursor.com/changelog/page/2)  
[https://cursor.com/changelog/page/4](https://cursor.com/changelog/page/4)  
[https://forum.cursor.com/t/update-available-whats-new/141859](https://forum.cursor.com/t/update-available-whats-new/141859)  
[https://releasebot.io/updates/cursor](https://releasebot.io/updates/cursor)

search_results.snippet: Explore perspectives and insights on the role of AI in product design and development. Learn helpful tips and how to incorporate it into your work.

message.content:
## Figma

**New products, capabilities, or features:** Figma Make and all Figma AI features (including Make, Edit Image, and Boost Resolution) have moved out of beta into general availability, now accessible to all users for generating, iterating, and building designs.

**Important updates, announcements, or changes:** 
- Full seat users can publish or privately share Figma Make files (publishing still in beta).
- View, Collab, and Dev seat users get unlimited drafts and relevant AI features.
- Starter plan users get unlimited drafts and can share up to 3 files with team.
- Other AI features limited to paid plans. AI usage now operates on credit limits, causing rapid consumption (e.g., 3000 credits in a day).

**User complaints:** Paid users report credit limits as not feasible, requesting unlimited usage under paid plans and local refinements without credit costs.

https://www.figma.com/blog/figma-make-general-availability/  
https://forum.figma.com/share-your-feedback-26/figma-make-ai-credit-limits-not-feasible-51713/index7.html  
https://www.figma.com/release-notes/  
https://www.figma.com/make/

## Competitor Monitoring Report (Last 30 Days)

### Lovable
No updates

### Bolt.new
No updates

### Replit
No updates

### V0 by Vercel
No updates

### Cursor
- Launched Cursor 3 as a unified workspace with agent-based development and a new higher‑level interface (source)[https://cursor.com/blog/cursor-3]
- Introduced tools for teams to configure development environments for agents (source)[https://cursor.com/changelog]
- Added 30+ partner plugins, including Atlassian, Datadog, GitLab, Glean, Hugging Face, monday.com, and PlanetScale (source)[https://cursor.com/changelog/page/4]
- Rolled out enterprise admin updates: model controls, improved spend management, and expanded usage details (source)[https://cursor.com/changelog/page/2]
- May 2026 updates summarized in an external timeline (source)[https://releasebot.io/updates/cursor]
- User complaints about frequent update prompts lacking clear “What’s New” details (source)[https://forum.cursor.com/t/update-available-whats-new/141859]

### Figma
- Announced general availability of Figma Make and Figma AI features (Make, Edit Image, Boost Resolution) for wider access (source)[https://www.figma.com/blog/figma-make-general-availability/]
- Updated seat and plan entitlements: full seats can publish/share Make files; View/Collab/Dev seats and Starter get unlimited drafts, with AI features varying by plan (source)[https://www.figma.com/blog/figma-make-general-availability/]
- Shifted AI usage to credit-based limits; users report rapid credit consumption and request more generous or unlimited paid usage (source)[https://forum.figma.com/share-your-feedback-26/figma-make-ai-credit-limits-not-feasible-51713/index7.html]
